chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. Bail Appl.No.1068 of 2026 Date Order with signature of the Judge Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro. Mr. Justice Khalid Hussain Shahani. Shahrukh..............Vs........................ The State 04.05.2026. Ms. Farzana, Advocate for applicant Mr. Khadim Hussain, Addl. P.G. O R D E R = MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Applicant was arrested by a police team of P.S. SIU, Karachi from main road opposite Gulistan-e-Mustafa, Fire Station Sohrab Goth, Samanabad Karachi on 15.09.2025 and from him allegedly 30 grams of Cocaine was recovered which was duly video recorded on phone, hence he was booked in FIR bearing Cr. No.160/2025 U/s 9 7, Sindh CNS Amended Act, 2025 of P.S. SIU Karachi. 2. Learned counsel for applicant has pleaded for bail citing that in Chemical report net weight of recovered quantity of Cocaine is shown as 03 grams instead of 30 grams, which fact is also mentioned in the Challan, hence the case against the applicant requires further inquiry. 3. On the other hand, learned Addl. P.G. has opposed the bail but has confirmed that in Chemical report as well as in Challan, net weight of recovered Cocaine has been opined as 03 grams. We, therefore, are of the view that prima facie finding in Chemical report as well as in Challan qua net weight of recovered Cocaine as 03 grams makes the case against the applicant to be one of further inquiry. Learned trial judge while dismissing the bail application has not even taken into account this fact nor discussed it. We, therefore, allow this application and grant bail to the applicant subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trail court.

Learned trial judge while dismissing the bail application has not even taken into account this fact nor discussed it. We, therefore, allow this application and grant bail to the applicant subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trail court. Office is directed to send a copy of this order to the relevant Sessions Judge for his guidance in future. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. The Cr. Bail Application is disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE A.K
